query_text: Is Observation.code required in FHIR R4B?
answer: Yes. In FHIR R4B, Observation.code is required (cardinality 1..1), so an Observation must include a code describing what was observed.

query_text: I have Observation.valueQuantity but no UCUM unit code; can I just put "mg per dl" as text?
answer: You can populate Quantity.unit as text, so base FHIR may still validate structurally. But for interoperable lab-style data, you should use UCUM in Quantity.system='http://unitsofmeasure.org' and Quantity.code (typically 'mg/dL'). Free text like 'mg per dl' is weaker and may fail profile-level validation.

query_text: Does validating with fhir-resource-diff guarantee US Core conformance?
answer: No. It can help with core FHIR structure/content checks, but US Core conformance requires validating against US Core profiles, invariants, and terminology bindings (typically with an IG-aware validator and the US Core package).

query_text: Compare what changed in MedicationRequest between R4 and R5 that affects field requirements.
answer: Key requirement-impacting change: R4's medicationCodeableConcept|medicationReference became a single required element in R5, medication (CodeableReference), still required but modeled differently. Also, several paired code/reference patterns were harmonized in R5, which affects mappings even when cardinalities stay similar. Core required backbone remains broadly similar (for example, status and intent still required), but migration work is mostly around datatype and element-shape changes.

query_text: Here's a Patient JSON missing resourceType. What is the minimum fix and why?
answer: Minimum fix: add "resourceType": "Patient" at the top level. Why: resourceType is mandatory for all FHIR resources and tells parsers and validators which resource schema to apply.

query_text: Write a PostgreSQL query to find duplicate emails in users table.
answer: SELECT email, COUNT(*) AS occurrences FROM users GROUP BY email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

query_text: Explain the difference between React Server Components and client components.
answer: Server Components render on the server, can access server-only resources directly, and reduce client JavaScript sent to the browser. Client Components run in the browser, support state/effects/event handlers, and are needed for interactivity.

query_text: Draft a polite follow-up email after a missed interview.
answer:
Subject: Follow-up on Interview

Hi [Name],

I wanted to follow up regarding our interview scheduled for [date/time]. I understand schedules can shift, and I am still very interested in the opportunity.

If helpful, I am happy to reschedule at your convenience.

Best,
[Your Name]

query_text: What's the fastest way to zip a folder on macOS terminal?

answer: Mon: Full body. Tue: Light cardio + mobility. Wed: Full body. Thu: Light cardio + mobility. Fri: Full body. Keep weights easy-moderate and progress slowly.
